title: Lochnera pusilla
taxonomy: Lochnera pusilla is classified as a plant in the family Rhamnaceae. It belongs to the genus Lochnera within the family Rhamnaceae. The family Rhamnaceae is a group of flowering plants commonly known as buckthorn or dogwood family. This plant is often classified as a species of flowering plant in the order Rosales. Lochnera pusilla is native to tropical Africa.
uses: Lochnera pusilla is known for its traditional medicinal uses, particularly in South America, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. intro: Lochnera pusilla, also known as the dwarf Lochnera, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Gentianaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and digestive disorders. The bioactive constituents of Lochnera pusilla include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
